mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll growth signals strong labor market demand, increasing corporate earnings visibility and driving S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when PAYEMS grows >100k/month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate improving consumer confidence and business investment, creating positive momentum in equity markets over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 40%+ of periods when HOUST increases >5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged increase in CPI above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures, prompting central bank tightening that reduces equity multiples and causes SPX negative returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rising oil prices signal supply chain disruptions, creating stagflationary risks that drive SPX negative returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods when INDPRO declines >1% and CPIAUCSL rises >0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth below 2% signals weakening economic demand, leading to SPX negative returns over 60 trading days during late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods when GDPC1 growth falls below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate below 3.5% signals labor market tightness, increasing wage pressures that reduce corporate margins and drive SPX negative returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods when UNRATE falls below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in consumer credit (RSAFS) above $500B combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates excessive demand, creating equity market overvaluation and negative SPX returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 40%+ of periods when RSAFS >500B and HOUST >1200k.
